verses.text: What advantage hath the Jew? much; 'unto them were committed the oracles of God'; 'let God be true.' Trusted with the word. Their unbelief cannot make God false.
What advantage hath the Jew? much; 'unto them were committed the oracles of God'; 'let God be true.' Trusted with the word. Their unbelief cannot make God false.
What advantage hath the Jew? much; 'unto them were committed the oracles of God'; 'let God be true.' Trusted with the word. Their unbelief cannot make God false.
What advantage hath the Jew? much; 'unto them were committed the oracles of God'; 'let God be true.' Trusted with the word. Their unbelief cannot make God false.
Is God unrighteous to take vengeance? God forbid; else how shall He judge the world? God just. His justice is the ground of all judgment.
Is God unrighteous to take vengeance? God forbid; else how shall He judge the world? God just. His justice is the ground of all judgment.
Is God unrighteous to take vengeance? God forbid; else how shall He judge the world? God just. His justice is the ground of all judgment.
Is God unrighteous to take vengeance? God forbid; else how shall He judge the world? God just. His justice is the ground of all judgment.
Jews and Gentiles are all under sin; 'There is none righteous, no, not one.' Universal guilt. The whole world stands condemned before God.
Jews and Gentiles are all under sin; 'There is none righteous, no, not one.' Universal guilt. The whole world stands condemned before God.
Jews and Gentiles are all under sin; 'There is none righteous, no, not one.' Universal guilt. The whole world stands condemned before God.
Jews and Gentiles are all under sin; 'There is none righteous, no, not one.' Universal guilt. The whole world stands condemned before God.
Their throat 'an open sepulchre,' their feet 'swift to shed blood.' Corrupt speech and deeds. Sin defiles word and way alike.
Their throat 'an open sepulchre,' their feet 'swift to shed blood.' Corrupt speech and deeds. Sin defiles word and way alike.
Their throat 'an open sepulchre,' their feet 'swift to shed blood.' Corrupt speech and deeds. Sin defiles word and way alike.
Destruction in their ways; 'the way of peace have they not known'; 'no fear of God before their eyes.' Lost peace. The root of all is no fear of God.
Destruction in their ways; 'the way of peace have they not known'; 'no fear of God before their eyes.' Lost peace. The root of all is no fear of God.
Destruction in their ways; 'the way of peace have they not known'; 'no fear of God before their eyes.' Lost peace. The root of all is no fear of God.
By the law is 'the knowledge of sin'; by its deeds 'shall no flesh be justified.' The law's work. It exposes sin but cannot save.
By the law is 'the knowledge of sin'; by its deeds 'shall no flesh be justified.' The law's work. It exposes sin but cannot save.
Now the righteousness of God by faith is shown; 'all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.' All fall short. None can stand on his own merit.
Now the righteousness of God by faith is shown; 'all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.' All fall short. None can stand on his own merit.
Now the righteousness of God by faith is shown; 'all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.' All fall short. None can stand on his own merit.
'Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,' set forth a propitiation. Free grace. Christ's blood answers both mercy and justice.
'Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,' set forth a propitiation. Free grace. Christ's blood answers both mercy and justice.
'Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,' set forth a propitiation. Free grace. Christ's blood answers both mercy and justice.
Boasting is excluded; 'a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.' By faith alone. Salvation leaves no room for human pride.
Boasting is excluded; 'a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.' By faith alone. Salvation leaves no room for human pride.
He is the God of Gentiles also; do we void the law by faith? 'we establish the law.' One way for all. Faith honours, not abolishes, the law.
